title: hoya pubicorolla subsp. anthracina
intro: Hoya pubicorolla subsp. anthracina, also known as Black Hoya, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family. It is a climbing vine native to the tropical regions of the Philippines. The medicinal properties of Hoya pubicorolla subsp. anthracina include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal preparations to promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress, making it a popular remedy for overall well-being.
